top_trades.reason: Largest premium trade at $1.55M with 546 contracts, these ITM calls 11% below current price show strong bullish conviction with over a year to expiration. The massive size and deep ITM positioning suggests institutional accumulation or a delta-one replacement strategy expecting AMD to maintain or grow from current levels through April 2026.

Over $1M premium on deeply ITM calls (38% ITM) expiring March 2026 represents a synthetic long stock position with significant capital commitment. The buyer is essentially locking in exposure to AMD with massive downside protection, paying around $80/contract for calls that behave like stock with minimal time decay risk.

Massive 1,704 contract position (largest size in the data) on slightly OTM calls just 4% above current price with two years to expiration. Despite lower individual premium (~$153/contract), the enormous size signals major bullish positioning expecting AMZN to exceed $222.50, requiring only modest 4% appreciation.

High premium ($773K) on deep ITM TSLA calls 25% below spot price with March 2026 expiration shows conviction in maintaining elevated levels. At roughly $10,450 per contract with tight $3.80 spread, this represents sophisticated positioning capturing delta exposure while limiting capital outlay versus owning 7,400 shares.

Nearly $785K in ITM put protection 23% above current AMD price suggests either hedging a large long position or outright bearish positioning expecting significant downside. The strike implies AMD would need to fall substantially, but the buyer is paying hefty premium ($4,935/contract) for this long-dated March 2026 protection.
